notes: Converted from the old chapter extraction schema.
Hidden facts, motives, and faction pressures require living-world re-extraction.
Merged second-stage living-world enrichment; still requires expert/manual review.

insufficient source-backed seed: The seed package describes an open battle scene between Heavenly Court Gu Immortals and Northern Plains historical experts summoned by Hong Lian Demon Venerable's move 'There Were People Before'. All events are publicly observable combat actions. No private knowledge, secret motives/plans, future-known information, or directly supported knowledge gaps are present in the seed. The entities' visibility is marked 'ambiguous' but the source text only shows them fighting openly. No hidden fact candidate can be extracted without speculating beyond the source.
Merged second-stage living-world enrichment; still requires expert/manual review.

This package is a large-scale battle scene with multiple combatants. All observed behaviors are combat actions (attacking, blocking, countering) during a chaotic battle. Motives are ambiguous - characters are fighting because they are on opposing sides (天庭 vs 红莲魔尊's summoned北原强者), but the seed only shows immediate tactical actions, not personal motives or strategic intent. No single character has enough behavioral evidence to infer a non-obvious motive beyond 'fighting the enemy'. Returning empty.
Merged second-stage living-world enrichment; still requires expert/manual review.

insufficient source-backed seed: The seed describes a large-scale battle between individual Heavenly Court Gu Immortals (龙公, 肉鞭仙, 张飞熊, 朱雀儿, 玉珠子, 野樵子, 旋空童子, 周雄信, 万紫红, 秦松) and summoned Northern Plains historical Gu Immortals (努尔暴雄, 东方玉, 赵山河, 廿二农夫, 耶律寇, 袁贲, 冰塞川). All actors are individuals fighting in a chaotic melee. No collective faction/group pressure dynamics are established — there is no named clan, family, shop, hall, or organization with a concrete stake beyond '天庭蛊仙' as a loose affiliation label. The seed does not tie any individual's stake to a family, clan, or organizational interest. No faction-level pressure axis (interest, threat, resource, reputation, geography, succession, inheritance) can be derived from individual combat actions alone.
IF export filter quarantined 7 legacy old-schema branches.
